qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: HTTP C2 listener reads up to 8 MB from every unauthenticated request before key-exchange validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding reports pre-validation request-body allocation in server/c2/http.go:startSessionHandler, while the advisory concerns a nil pointer dereference in server/handlers/sessions.go:tunnelCloseHandler when closing reverse tunnels.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying final finding identifies the advisory-described reverse-tunnel close bug. The advisory root cause is dereferencing tunnel.SessionID in tunnelCloseHandler after core.Tunnels.Get returned nil and rtunnels.GetRTunnel returned a reverse tunnel; the only materialized qualifying finding is an unrelated HTTP C2 request-body allocation denial of service in startSessionHandler.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-c279-989m-238f identifies server/handlers/sessions.go:tunnelCloseHandler lines 172 and 175, where tunnel.SessionID is dereferenced in the reverse-tunnel close branch even though tunnel is nil.
http-c2-unauth-body-dos/vulnerability_report.json identifies server/c2/http.go:startSessionHandler and io.ReadAll on an unauthenticated request body before key-exchange validation.
Session .judge/input_manifest.json lists http-c2-unauth-body-dos as the only materialized qualifying_vuln finding.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 do not apply.
